message: >=3 failing tool results accumulated in the recent window — whether one failure is recurring or several distinct things are breaking, the task is stalling, which is fable-mode's trigger ("a task keeps failing or stalling", "debugging where the first theory might be wrong"). Before the next attempt, apply the five-gate loop: (1) restate the goal/scope, (2) re-gather evidence about WHY each failure happens (the tool's contract, the actual state, the exact error text — don't lump distinct failures together), (3) adversarially falsify your current theory — if two attempts at the same fix failed, the diagnosis is wrong, (4) verify the fix fresh, (5) report calibrated. Load skills/fable-mode/SKILL.md if not already applied.
_note: H2a mechanical-evidence probe for the default-on fable-mode skill (previously SKILL.md only — description-triggered, zero mechanical firing signal; closes the banked lesson 'make skills fire via mechanical probes, not description tuning'). MECHANISM, stated honestly: detect_repeated_tool_error COUNTS is_error tool_results matching the pattern (>=min_count in the recent window); it does NOT group by signature and cannot tell 'same failure 3x' from '3 unrelated failures' — so the message claims only 'the task is stalling', which is true in both cases, and the prescription (re-scope, re-evidence per-failure, falsify the theory) is correct in both cases. An earlier draft claimed 'the same class of failure recurred — stop retrying variations'; an adversarial review proved that false-diagnosis on 3 distinct errors (it would advise stopping variation exactly when variation is right) — locked as a lesson: a probe's message must not assert semantics its detector cannot observe. COMPLEMENTARY to compliance-canary's always-on trajectory_drift (rate errs/calls>=0.25 over >=8 calls): this fires on absolute COUNT >=3 at any rate (verified: 3 matching errors in 20 calls fires here, silent there; 3 non-matching errors in 8 calls fires there, silent here). SCOPE OF VALIDITY (LEARNING_CONTRACT §3): pattern covers common English failure text incl. segfault/core-dump/timeout/permission-denied/connection-refused/exit(ed)-with-code-N/expected-X-got-Y; still blind to keyword-free failures — bare 'Killed' (OOM; too collisive a word to match safely), non-English messages, and exit-0-but-wrong-output. The silent-wrong-output class is UNDETECTABLE from is_error results by construction (nothing errored) — that clause of fable-mode's trigger, like the 'many-layers/unknowns' clause, remains description-triggered only. Advisory heuristic; false positives (benign text containing failure words in >=3 genuinely-errored results) accepted at severity=warn.
